                                  X 
                               
                            
                          implies that  
                            
                               ϕ 
                               
                                  
                                     x 
                                     , 
                                     y 
                                  
                               
                               = 
                               ϕ 
                               
                                  
                                     x 
                                     + 
                                     δ 
                                     , 
                                     y 
                                     + 
                                     δ 
                                  
                               
                            
                         . By letting  
                            
                               δ 
                               = 
                               − 
                               y 
                            
                         , we obtain that  
                            
                               ϕ 
                               
                                  
                                     x 
                                     , 
                                     y 
                                  
                               
                               = 
                               ϕ 
                               
                                  
                                     x 
                                     − 
                                     y 
                                     , 
                                     0 
                                  
                               
                            
                          for all  
                            
                               x 
                               , 
                               y 
                               ∈ 
                               X 
                            
                         . Define  
                            
                               η 
                               
                                  
                                     x 
                                     − 
                                     y 
                                  
                               
                               = 
                               ϕ 
                               
                                  
                                     x 
                                     − 
                                     y 
                                     , 
                                     0 
                                  
                               
                            
                          for all  
                            
                               x 
                               , 
                               y 
                               ∈ 
                               X 
                            
                         . Since  
                            ϕ 
                          is continuous, monotonic, and skew-symmetric,  
                            η 
                          is continuous, strictly increasing, and odd on  
                            
                               
                                  Δ 
                               
                               
                                  X 
                               
                            
                         .

Since the probabilistically sophisticated regret-freeness on  
                            
                               
                                  Δ 
                               
                               
                                  X 
                               
                            
                          is too restrictive to derive the decomposition  (6) , we weaken the concept by restricting the set of regret-free pairs of acts to the subset whose elements satisfy probability-equivalence as shown by the following axiom.

Axiom B1 .  For all  
                         
                            
                               f 
                               , 
                               g 
                               ∈ 
                               
                                  
                                     F 
                                  
                                  
                                     s 
                                  
                               
                               
                                  
                                     X 
                                  
                               
                            
                         ,  if  
                         
                            f 
                           and  
                         
                            g 
                           are probability-equivalent on  
                         
                            X 
                           and regret-free on  
                         
                            
                               
                                  Δ 
                               
                               
                                  X 
                               
                            
                         
                         , then  
                         
                            
                               f 
                               ∼ 
                               g 
                            
                         .

The representational implication of Axiom B1 is given by the following theorem, whose lengthy proof will be deferred to  Appendix .

Theorem 4 
                            
                               Suppose that  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        ϕ 
                                        , 
                                        π 
                                     
                                  
                                
                               is a continuous and monotonic SSA representation of  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        F 
                                        
                                           
                                              X 
                                           
                                        
                                        , 
                                        ≿ 
                                     
                                  
                               
                               with  
                               
                                  X 
                                
                               a nondegenerate, bounded, and closed interval. Then Axiom B1 holds if and only if there exist a signed-threshold decomposition  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        u 
                                        , 
                                        η 
                                     
                                  
                                
                               of  
                               
                                  ϕ 
                               
                               for which at least one of  
                               
                                  u 
                                
                               and  
                               
                                  η 
                               
                               must be strictly increasing. Furthermore, if  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        v 
                                        , 
                                        τ 
                                     
                                  
                                
                               is a signed-threshold decomposition of  
                               
                                  ϕ 
                               
                               , then there are real numbers  
                               
                                  
                                     α 
                                     > 
                                     0 
                                  
                               
                               ,  
                               
                                  β 
                               
                               , and  
                               
                                  γ 
                                
                               such that, for all  
                               
                                  
                                     x 
                                     ∈ 
                                     X 
                                  
                                
                               and all  
                               
                                  
                                     λ 
                                     ∈ 
                                     
                                        
                                           Δ 
                                        
                                        
                                           X 
                                        
                                     
                                  
                               
                               ,  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        
                                           
                                              v 
                                              
                                                 
                                                    x 
                                                 
                                              
                                              = 
                                              α 
                                              u 
                                              
                                                 
                                                    x 
                                                 
                                              
                                              + 
                                              β 
                                              x 
                                              + 
                                              γ 
                                              , 
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                     
                                        
                                           
                                              τ 
                                              
                                                 
                                                    λ 
                                                 
                                              
                                              = 
                                              α 
                                              η 
                                              
                                                 
                                                    λ 
                                                 
                                              
                                              − 
                                              β 
                                              λ 
                                              .

Suppose that  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        ϕ 
                                        , 
                                        π 
                                     
                                  
                                
                               is a continuous and monotonic SSA representation of  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        F 
                                        
                                           
                                              X 
                                           
                                        
                                        , 
                                        ≿ 
                                     
                                  
                               
                               with  
                               
                                  X 
                                
                               a nondegenerate, bounded, and closed interval. Then Axiom B1 holds if and only if there exist a signed-threshold decomposition  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        u 
                                        , 
                                        η 
                                     
                                  
                                
                               of  
                               
                                  ϕ 
                               
                               for which at least one of  
                               
                                  u 
                                
                               and  
                               
                                  η 
                               
                               must be strictly increasing. Furthermore, if  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        v 
                                        , 
                                        τ 
                                     
                                  
                                
                               is a signed-threshold decomposition of  
                               
                                  ϕ 
                               
                               , then there are real numbers  
                               
                                  
                                     α 
                                     > 
                                     0 
                                  
                               
                               ,  
                               
                                  β 
                               
                               , and  
                               
                                  γ 
                                
                               such that, for all  
                               
                                  
                                     x 
                                     ∈ 
                                     X 
                                  
                                
                               and all  
                               
                                  
                                     λ 
                                     ∈ 
                                     
                                        
                                           Δ 
                                        
                                        
                                           X 
                                        
                                     
                                  
                               
                               ,  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        
                                           
                                              v 
                                              
                                                 
                                                    x 
                                                 
                                              
                                              = 
                                              α 
                                              u 
                                              
                                                 
                                                    x 
                                                 
                                              
                                              + 
                                              β 
                                              x 
                                              + 
                                              γ 
                                              , 
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                     
                                        
                                           
                                              τ 
                                              
                                                 
                                                    λ 
                                                 
                                              
                                              = 
                                              α 
                                              η 
                                              
                                                 
                                                    λ 
                                                 
                                              
                                              − 
                                              β 
                                              λ 
                                              .

Although the boundedness and closedness of  
                            X 
                          can be removed in  Theorem 4 , we adopted those assumptions since the essential idea of the proof method is unchanged and extra technical complications can be avoided.

The simplest examples of pairs of acts required by Axiom B1 are described in  Table 1 . The first example (a) augments half of the six outcomes, say  
                            
                               x 
                               , 
                               y 
                            
                         , and  
                            z 
                         , common for  
                            f 
                          and  
                            g 
                         , by an amount  
                            δ 
                         , but those acts exchange the augmented outcomes. This exchange augmentation imposes regret-freeness on the monetary domain. In the second example (b),  
                            x 
                          and  
                            y 
                          may be interpreted as amounts of deviation from a fixed amount  
                            z 
                         . Then outcomes of each act deviate from  
                            z 
                          by the amounts of  
                            
                               0 
                               , 
                               x 
                            
                         , and  
                            y 
                          with equal probabilities, but also they deviate to the opposite directions. This reflective deviations impose regret-freeness. Probability-equivalence of  
                            f 
                          and  
                            g 
                          in the both example clearly holds true.

If  
                            
                               
                                  ϕ 
                                  , 
                                  π 
                               
                            
                          is a continuous and monotonic SSA representation of  
                            
                               
                                  F 
                                  
                                     
                                        X 
                                     
                                  
                                  , 
                                  ≿ 
                               
                            
                         , then any pairs of acts in  Table 1  (a) and (b) respectively satisfy the following equations of  
                            ϕ 
                         , which are understood as applying to all  
                            
                               x 
                               , 
                               y 
                               , 
                               z 
                               , 
                               x 
                               + 
                               δ 
                               , 
                               y 
                               + 
                               δ 
                               , 
                               z 
                               + 
                               δ 
                               ∈ 
                               X 
                            
                         .

(a)  
                            
                               ϕ 
                               
                                  
                                     x 
                                     , 
                                     y 
                                  
                               
                               + 
                               ϕ 
                               
                                  
                                     y 
                                     , 
                                     z 
                                  
                               
                               + 
                               ϕ 
                               
                                  
                                     z 
                                     , 
                                     x 
                                  
                               
                               = 
                               ϕ 
                               
                                  
                                     x 
                                     + 
                                     δ 
                                     , 
                                     y 
                                     + 
                                     δ 
                                  
                               
                               + 
                               ϕ 
                               
                                  
                                     y 
                                     + 
                                     δ 
                                     , 
                                     z 
                                     + 
                                     δ 
                                  
                               
                               + 
                               ϕ 
                               
                                  
                                     z 
                                     + 
                                     δ 
                                     , 
                                     x 
                                     + 
                                     δ 
                                  
                               
                            
                         ,

(b)  
                            
                               ϕ 
                               
                                  
                                     z 
                                     , 
                                     z 
                                     + 
                                     x 
                                  
                               
                               + 
                               ϕ 
                               
                                  
                                     z 
                                     + 
                                     x 
                                     , 
                                     z 
                                     + 
                                     y 
                                  
                               
                               + 
                               ϕ 
                               
                                  
                                     z 
                                     + 
                                     y 
                                     , 
                                     z 
                                  
                               
                               = 
                               ϕ 
                               
                                  
                                     z 
                                     − 
                                     x 
                                     , 
                                     z 
                                  
                               
                               + 
                               ϕ 
                               
                                  
                                     z 
                                     − 
                                     y 
                                     , 
                                     z 
                                     − 
                                     x 
                                  
                               
                               + 
                               ϕ 
                               
                                  
                                     z 
                                     , 
                                     z 
                                     − 
                                     y 
                                  
                               
                            
                         .

The proof idea of  Theorem 4  is to show that the solution which satisfies both of (a) and (b) is given by  (6) . In particular, only (a) will be used to construct a continuous and increasing function  
                            u 
                          on  
                            X 
                          and a continuous, increasing, and odd function  
                            η 
                          on  
                            
                               
                                  Δ 
                               
                               
                                  X 
                               
                            
                         , in  (6)  when  
                            
                               X 
                               = 
                               
                                  
                                     0 
                                     , 
                                     1 
                                  
                               
                            
                         . When  
                            X 
                          is an open interval, we may apply (b) to construct  
                            u 
                          and  
                            η 
                          on the entire domains.

Although the representational implication of  Theorem 4  assures increasingness of both of  
                            u 
                          and  
                            η 
                         , it seems to be undesirable that  
                            u 
                          is constant on some interval in  
                            X 
                          while constancy of  
                            η 
                          may not be. The following example shows that for a signed-threshold decomposition  
                            
                               
                                  u 
                                  , 
                                  η 
                               
                            
                          of  
                            ϕ 
                         , either one of  
                            u 
                          and  
                            η 
                          is strictly increasing, but both of them cannot be transformed to be strictly increasing.

Example 1 
                            Assume  
                                  
                                     X 
                                     = 
                                     
                                        
                                           0 
                                           , 
                                           1 
                                        
                                     
                                  
                               . Let  
                                  
                                     
                                        u 
                                        , 
                                        η 
                                     
                                  
                                be a signed-threshold decomposition of  
                                  ϕ 
                               . Define  
                                  u 
                                on  
                                  X 
                                and  
                                  η 
                                on  
                                  
                                     
                                        Δ 
                                     
                                     
                                        X 
                                     
                                  
                                as follows:  
                                  
                                     
                                        
                                           
                                              u 
                                              
                                                 
                                                    x 
                                                 
                                              
                                              = 
                                              
                                                 
                                                    
                                                       
                                                          
                                                             0 
                                                          
                                                          
                                                             
                                                                
                                                                   0 
                                                                   ≤ 
                                                                   x 
                                                                   ≤ 
                                                                   
                                                                      
                                                                         1 
                                                                      
                                                                      
                                                                         3 
                                                                      
                                                                   
                                                                
                                                             
                                                             , 
                                                          
                                                       
                                                       
                                                          
                                                             
                                                                
                                                                   3 
                                                                
                                                                
                                                                   2 
                                                                
                                                             
                                                             x 
                                                             − 
                                                             
                                                                
                                                                   1 
                                                                
                                                                
                                                                   2 
                                                                
                                                             
                                                          
                                                          
                                                             
                                                                
                                                                   
                                                                      
                                                                         1 
                                                                      
                                                                      
                                                                         3 
                                                                      
                                                                   
                                                                   < 
                                                                   x 
                                                                   ≤ 
                                                                   1 
                                                                
                                                             
                                                             , 
                                                          
                                                       
                                                    
                                                 
                                              
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                     
                                        
                                           
                                              η 
                                              
                                                 
                                                    λ 
                                                 
                                              
                                              = 
                                              
                                                 
                                                    
                                                       
                                                          
                                                             
                                                                
                                                                   λ 
                                                                
                                                                
                                                                   2 
                                                                
                                                             
                                                          
                                                          
                                                             
                                                                
                                                                   0 
                                                                   ≤ 
                                                                   λ 
                                                                   < 
                                                                   1 
                                                                
                                                             
                                                             , 
                                                          
                                                       
                                                       
                                                          
                                                             − 
                                                             
                                                                
                                                                   λ 
                                                                
                                                                
                                                                   2 
                                                                
                                                             
                                                          
                                                          
                                                             
                                                                
                                                                   − 
                                                                   1 
                                                                   ≤ 
                                                                   λ 
                                                                   < 
                                                                   0 
                                                                
                                                             
                                                             . 
                                                          
                                                       
                                                    
                                                 
                                              
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                  
                                Then  
                                  u 
                                is not strictly increasing, but increasing. By the uniqueness in  Theorem 4 ,  
                                  
                                     
                                        w 
                                        , 
                                        τ 
                                     
                                  
                                is another signed-threshold decomposition of  
                                  ϕ 
                                if and only if  
                                  
                                     w 
                                     
                                        
                                           x 
                                        
                                     
                                     = 
                                     α 
                                     u 
                                     
                                        
                                           x 
                                        
                                     
                                     + 
                                     β 
                                     x 
                                     + 
                                     γ 
                                  
                                and  
                                  
                                     τ 
                                     
                                        
                                           λ 
                                        
                                     
                                     = 
                                     α 
                                     η 
                                     
                                        
                                           λ 
                                        
                                     
                                     − 
                                     β 
                                     λ 
                                  
                                for some numbers  
                                  
                                     α 
                                     > 
                                     0 
                                  
                               ,  
                                  β 
                               , and  
                                  γ 
                               . For  
                                  w 
                                to be strictly increasing, we must have  
                               
                                  
                                     β 
                                     > 
                                     0 
                                  
                               . However, in this case,  
                                  τ 
                                becomes strictly decreasing for all  
                                  x 
                                which are close to  
                                  0 
                               . Hence, there exists no signed-threshold decomposition of  
                                  ϕ 
                               , whose component functions are strictly increasing. □

Assume  
                                  
                                     X 
                                     = 
                                     
                                        
                                           0 
                                           , 
                                           1 
                                        
                                     
                                  
                               . Let

                                                                   2 
                                                                
                                                             
                                                          
                                                          
                                                             
                                                                
                                                                   0

                                                             . 
                                                          
                                                       
                                                    
                                                 
                                              
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                  
                                Then  
                                  u

                                        w 
                                        , 
                                        τ 
                                     
                                  
                                is another signed-threshold decomposition of  
                                  ϕ 
                                if and only if  
                                  
                                     w 
                                     
                                        
                                           x 
                                        
                                     
                                     = 
                                     α 
                                     u

                                     + 
                                     β

                                     α

                                     λ 
                                  
                                for some numbers  
                                  
                                     α 
                                     > 
                                     0 
                                  
                               ,  
                                  β 
                               , and  
                                  γ 
                               . For  
                                  w 
                                to be strictly increasing, we must have  
                               
                                  
                                     β 
                                     > 
                                     0 
                                  
                               . However, in this case,  
                                  τ 
                                becomes strictly decreasing for all  
                                  x 
                                which are close to  
                                  0 
                               . Hence, there exists no signed-threshold decomposition of  
                                  ϕ 
                               , whose component functions are strictly increasing. □

Contrary to convexity of  
                            u 
                          in  Example 1 , if  
                            u 
                          is concave, it is always possible to make  
                            u 
                          strictly increasing. Concavity of  
                            u 
                          is characterized by the following condition.

Condition 1 
                            For all  
                                  
                                     x 
                                     , 
                                     y 
                                     , 
                                     z 
                                     ∈ 
                                     X 
                                  
                                 and  
                               
                                  
                                     A 
                                     ∈ 
                                     
                                        
                                           2 
                                        
                                        
                                           S 
                                        
                                     
                                  
                               ,  
                                  
                                     z 
                                     ≿ 
                                     
                                        
                                           x 
                                        
                                        
                                           A 
                                        
                                     
                                     y 
                                  
                                
                               whenever  
                               
                                  
                                     x 
                                     > 
                                     y 
                                  
                                 and  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        
                                           x 
                                        
                                        
                                           A 
                                        
                                     
                                     y 
                                  
                                  and  
                               
                                  z 
                                 are regret-free on  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        Δ 
                                     
                                     
                                        X 
                                     
                                  
                               . 
                            To see that  Condition 1  is necessary and sufficient for concavity of  
                                  u 
                               , let  
                                  
                                     
                                        u 
                                        , 
                                        η 
                                     
                                  
                                be a signed-threshold decomposition of  
                                  ϕ 
                               , and assume that  
                                  
                                     x 
                                     > 
                                     y 
                                  
                                and  
                                  
                                     
                                        
                                           x 
                                        
                                        
                                           A 
                                        
                                     
                                     y 
                                  
                                and  
                                  z 
                                are regret-free on  
                                  
                                     
                                        Δ 
                                     
                                     
                                        X 
                                     
                                  
                               . Then  
                                  
                                     x 
                                     − 
                                     z 
                                     = 
                                     z 
                                     − 
                                     y 
                                  
                                and  
                                  
                                     π 
                                     
                                        
                                           A 
                                        
                                     
                                     = 
                                     
                                        
                                           1 
                                        
                                        
                                           2 
                                        
                                     
                                  
                               . Thus  
                                  
                                     
                                        
                                           z 
                                           ≿ 
                                           
                                              
                                                 x 
                                              
                                              
                                                 A 
                                              
                                           
                                           y 
                                           
                                           ⟺ 
                                           
                                           ϕ 
                                           
                                              
                                                 z 
                                                 , 
                                                 x 
                                              
                                           
                                           + 
                                           ϕ 
                                           
                                              
                                                 z 
                                                 , 
                                                 y 
                                              
                                           
                                           ≥ 
                                           0 
                                           
                                           ⟺ 
                                           
                                           u 
                                           
                                              
                                                 z 
                                              
                                           
                                           − 
                                           u 
                                           
                                              
                                                 x 
                                              
                                           
                                           + 
                                           η 
                                           
                                              
                                                 z 
                                                 − 
                                                 x 
                                              
                                           
                                           + 
                                           u 
                                           
                                              
                                                 z 
                                              
                                           
                                           − 
                                           u 
                                           
                                              
                                                 y 
                                              
                                           
                                           
                                           + 
                                           
                                           η 
                                           
                                              
                                                 z 
                                                 − 
                                                 y 
                                              
                                           
                                           ≥ 
                                           0 
                                           
                                           ⟺ 
                                           
                                           u 
                                           
                                              
                                                 
                                                    
                                                       x 
                                                       + 
                                                       y 
                                                    
                                                    
                                                       2 
                                                    
                                                 
                                              
                                           
                                           ≥ 
                                           
                                              
                                                 u 
                                                 
                                                    
                                                       x 
                                                    
                                                 
                                                 + 
                                                 u 
                                                 
                                                    
                                                       y 
                                                    
                                                 
                                              
                                              
                                                 2 
                                              
                                           
                                           . 
                                        
                                     
                                  
                               
                            
                            Finally, we comment on convexity of  
                                  η 
                               . It may be interpreted as in the literature for the classical simple regret model that convexity of  
                                  η 
                                on the positive domain as  
                                  η 
                                in  Example 1  exhibits regret aversion. The following condition ensures convexity of  
                                  η 
                               .

For all  
                                  
                                     x 
                                     , 
                                     y 
                                     , 
                                     z 
                                     ∈ 
                                     X 
                                  
                                 and  
                               
                                  
                                     A 
                                     ∈ 
                                     
                                        
                                           2 
                                        
                                        
                                           S 
                                        
                                     
                                  
                               ,  
                                  
                                     z 
                                     ≿ 
                                     
                                        
                                           x 
                                        
                                        
                                           A 
                                        
                                     
                                     y 
                                  
                                
                               whenever  
                               
                                  
                                     x 
                                     > 
                                     y 
                                  
                                 and  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        
                                           x 
                                        
                                        
                                           A 
                                        
                                     
                                     y 
                                  
                                  and  
                               
                                  z 
                                 are regret-free on  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        Δ 
                                     
                                     
                                        X 
                                     
                                  
                               .

To see that  Condition 1  is necessary and sufficient for concavity of  
                                  u 
                               , let  
                                  
                                     
                                        u 
                                        , 
                                        η 
                                     
                                  
                                be a signed-threshold decomposition of  
                                  ϕ 
                               , and assume that  
                                  
                                     x 
                                     > 
                                     y 
                                  
                                and  
                                  
                                     
                                        
                                           x 
                                        
                                        
                                           A 
                                        
                                     
                                     y 
                                  
                                and

The topological version is indeed stronger than an algebraic version in  Krantz et al. (1971)  who proved the existence of an SoP function by assuming solvability and Archimedean axioms (for the definitions, see Chapter 4 in  Krantz et al., 1971 ) in place of continuity in Axiom EP1. Although the proof of  Theorem 4  in Appendix is constructive, without consulting the proof method in depth, it may be worthwhile in this section to present and discuss how to quantitatively measure three functions  
                         v 
                      ,  
                         u 
                      , and  
                         η 
                       appeared in the signed-threshold decomposition  (3)  from the viewpoint of experimental studies. Data to construct  
                         u 
                       and  
                         η 
                       will be a collection of odds of events with which DM judges indifference between two binary lotteries. On the other hand, a  
                         v 
                      -scale must be independently measured. The hypothesis of the decomposition is that any two acts, which are probability equivalent on  
                         X 
                      , should be judged to be indifferent whenever balancing positive and negative  
                         v 
                      -differences of outcomes of those acts are judged to be equally probable. When we ignore balancing (respectively, probability-equivalence) aspect,  
                         η 
                       vanishes (respectively,  
                         v 
                       and  
                         u 
                       are cardinally equivalent). We examine three kinds of  
                         v 
                      -scales below. Which one of them is best suited to representing regret-freeness is due to an empirical matter.

                         v 
                      -scale on the set  
                         X 
                       of outcomes,

For a measurement purpose to generate an equally  
                         v 
                      -spaced outcomes, it may be better to introduce a mapping

                            X

The axiom system for

                               , 
                               μ 
                            
                         
                       to be represented by a cardinal  
                         v 
                      -scale is well known by  Pfanzagl ’s ( 1959 ) bisymmetric structure (see also Section 6.3 in  Fishburn, 1970  and Section 6.9 in  Krantz et al. (1971) ). This construction allows for different interpretations of  
                         μ 
                       values. Three of them may be given as follows:

(i) monetary mean values:  
                         
                            μ 
                            
                               
                                  x

Under interpretations (i) or (ii), they may not be indifferent because of risk aversion for (i) (see  Pratt, 1964 ) and relative risk aversion for (ii) (see  Dyer & Sarin, 1982; Keller, 1985; Krzysztofowicz, 1983 ), where those concepts are applied to the set of even-chance lotteries, since DM is not necessarily an SEU-maximizer. The aim of the paper was to develop axiom systems which guarantees the existence of an SEU representation with signed threshold. First, we explored a new axiom system for the SSA representation which drops both of transitivity axiom and Savage’s Axioms P2 and P3. Then we imposed two additional properties, continuity and monotonicity, on the SSA representation, where an ordinal scale  
                         v 
                      , which represents weakly ordered ex post preferences for outcomes, is introduced on the set of outcomes to rebuild the SSA representation on the range of  
                         v 
                      .

To arrive at a signed-threshold decomposition of the rebuilt SSA representation, it is shown to be necessary and sufficient that any pair of acts which are probability-equivalent and regret-free on an appropriate scale  
                         v 
                       should be indifferent. If such an appropriate scale meaningfully measures regret-freeness, it must be cardinal. The simplest is to measure regret-freeness by monetary differences, so  
                         v 
                       is assumed to be a linear function on the set of monetary outcomes. Thus  Theorem 4 , which establishes a signed-threshold decomposition on monetary outcomes, is the fundamental result for the decompositions derived by other possible cardinal scales.

In the literature, there may be proposed four approaches to construct a cardinal scale. They include strength of preferences (e.g., see  Suppes & Winet, 1955 ), expected utility (e.g., see  von Neumann & Morgenstern, 1947 , additive utility (e.g., see  Debreu, 1960 ), and the repetition approach (e.g., see  Camacho, 1979 ). In this paper, we adopted strength of preferences to demonstrate a construction method of  
                         v 
                       as a cardinal scale. The other two approaches excluding the repetition approach may be easily applied to our purpose. Applying expected utility to modeling ex post preferences is to simply introduce randomization of outcomes as in  Anscombe & Aumann ’s ( 1963 ) modeling. When the set  
                         X 
                       of outcomes is multiattributed, additive utility for ex post preferences gives a cardinal scale on  
                         X 
                      .

It may be desirable to construct  
                         v 
                       directly by appropriate conditions on ex ante preferences for acts. Although  Nakamura (2016)  devised such conditions under decision framework based on the concept of acceptability, which is quite different from the present paper, his conditions seem to be far from operational tractability. Thus, it may be an interesting open problem to pursue axiomatization of only ex ante preferences for acts in the present framework to derive three ingredients,  
                         v 
                      ,  
                         u 
                      , and  
                         η 
                      , of a signed threshold decomposition.

The author thanks two referees for valuable comments.

                             This completes the proof.  □

                       and

                       do not depend on fractional representations of the rationals.

Lemma A2 
                         
                            For all

The next three lemmas will establish that the defining Eq.  (A.2)  of  
                         u 
                       and  (A.4)  of  
                         η 
                       are in fact the ones required for a signed-threshold decomposition of  
                         ϕ 
                       on the set of all pairs of rationals. First, we show that three unknown parameters in the defining equations of  
                         u 
                       and  
                         f 
                       be dependent each other in a sense that only two of them can be arbitrary.

                             The second and third equalities respectively follow from  Lemmas A3 and A4 . When  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        m 
                                     
                                     
                                        n 
                                     
                                  
                                  <

                                              ′ 
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                     
                                        
                                           
                                              n 
                                           
                                           
                                              ′ 
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                  
                               
                            , the desire result follows from skew-symmetry of  
                               ϕ 
                             and oddity of  
                               η 
                            .  
                               
                                  
                                  □ 
                               
                            
                         
                         Now we prove the uniqueness of  
                               u 
                             and  
                               η 
                             on rationals.

                               □ 
                            
                         
                         So far, monotonicity properties of  
                               u 
                             and  
                               η 
                             did not matter. However, we can show that  
                               u 
                             and  
                               η 
                             on rationals can be always constructed to be increasing.

Let  
                               
                                  
                                     u 
                                     , 
                                     η

                               u

                               □

So far, monotonicity properties of  
                               u 
                             and  
                               η 
                             did not matter. However, we can show that  
                               u 
                             and  
                               η 
                             on rationals can be always constructed to be increasing.

Lemma A7 
                         
                            
                               u 
                             
                            and  
                            
                               η 
                             
                            on rationals can be made increasing. Furthermore, at least one of them can be made strictly increasing.

u 
                             
                            and  
                            
                               η 
                             
                            on rationals can be made increasing. Furthermore, at least one of them can be made strictly increasing.

Lemma A8 
                         
                            If

                         Since

Proof

                               x

                                              → 
                                              ∞ 
                                           
                                        
                                        ϕ 
                                        
                                           
                                              
                                                 
                                                    α

                                                    β

                                              − 
                                              u 
                                              
                                                 
                                                    
                                                       
                                                          β 
                                                       
                                                       
                                                          k 
                                                       
                                                    
                                                 
                                              
                                              + 
                                              η

                                        ϕ

                                        . 
                                     
                                  
                               
                            
                         
                         By  Lemma A8 ,  
                               
                                  lim 
                                  η

                            . Hence, we obtain the desired result. □ 
                         Now we extend  
                               u 
                             and

                             on rationals to all irrationals in  
                               X 
                            . For any irrational  
                               
                                  0 
                                  < 
                                  x

                                  1

                                              n 
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                  
                                  ⊂ 
                                  X 
                               
                            , which converges to  
                               x 
                            .  Lemma A9  assures that this definition is meaningful. Thus, by definition,  
                               u

                               X 
                            . 
                         By  Lemma A3 ,

                               u

                               η 
                             and  Lemma A6 . This completes the proof of  Theorem 4 . □
